Article.ArticleTitle: Remote monitoring contribution to the continuation of CPAP treatment in the long term: which gain and for whom? A cohort analysis.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Homedis Santé, 2 chemin des Ronzières, 69390, Vourles, France. jy.bayle@homedis.fr.
Hôpital Privé de l'Est Lyonnais, 140 rue André Lwoff, Saint-Priest, 69800, France.
INSERM U1018 & CESP, Université Paris-Saclay, Paris, France.
Hôpital Privé de l'Est Lyonnais, 140 rue André Lwoff, 69800, Saint-Priest, France.
INSERM U1028, Université Lyon, Lyon, France.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Ethical approval: The data collected here are totally anonymized CPAP rather than individual clinical data. In other words, this article does not contain any studies with human participants performed by any of the authors. According to French law, informed consent from individual patients or ethical committee approval is not required (Etude MR-004, hors Loi Jardé, N0.2012-300 of March 5, 2012). Conflict of interest: J-Y Bayle received fees from Homedis Santé company for consulting services and delivery of lectures. Homedis Santé had no role in the design or conduct of this research. S-H Onen and F Onen certify that they have no affiliations with or involvement in any organization or entity with any financial interest, or non-financial interest in the subject matter or materials discussed in this manuscript.
